title: CBS Boss Explains 'NCIS: New York' Over 'Tony & Ziva' Decision
body:
In a move that is set to shake up the television landscape, CBS has announced its decision to launch a new spinoff series, 'NCIS: New York', as part of its fall 2026 schedule. The news has sent shockwaves through the entertainment industry, with many fans and critics alike left wondering why the network opted for this new series over the highly anticipated 'Tony & Ziva' spinoff. According to CBS Entertainment President Amy Reisenbach, the decision to go with 'NCIS: New York' was a strategic one, driven by a desire to expand the NCIS franchise while also introducing a fresh perspective to the beloved series.

At the heart of 'NCIS: New York' is NCIS: Los Angeles star LL Cool J, who will reprise his role as NCIS Special Agent Sam Hanna. This move is seen as a clever play by CBS, as it allows the network to leverage the popularity of the NCIS franchise while also bringing a new energy to the series. By setting the show in New York, the network is also able to tap into the rich cultural and geographical diversity of the city, providing a unique backdrop for the show's storylines. With 'NCIS: New York', CBS is clearly looking to appeal to both longtime fans of the franchise and new viewers, who will be drawn in by the show's fresh setting and characters.

The decision to pass on 'Tony & Ziva' has been met with disappointment from some fans, who had been eagerly anticipating the spinoff series. However, according to Reisenbach, the network never seriously considered picking up the show for next season. This decision is likely due to the fact that 'Tony & Ziva' was still in the development stage, and CBS may have felt that it was not yet ready for primetime. Additionally, with 'NCIS: New York' already in the works, the network may have seen 'Tony & Ziva' as redundant, and opted to focus its resources on the new series instead.

The launch of 'NCIS: New York' is also significant in that it marks a continuation of CBS's strategy of expanding its successful franchises. In recent years, the network has seen huge success with spinoff series such as 'NCIS: Los Angeles' and 'NCIS: Hawaii', and it is clear that the network is looking to replicate this success with 'NCIS: New York'. By leveraging the popularity of the NCIS franchise, CBS is able to attract a built-in audience, while also providing a platform for new characters and storylines to emerge. This approach has proven to be highly effective for the network, and it will be interesting to see how 'NCIS: New York' performs in the ratings.

As the television landscape continues to evolve, it is clear that CBS is committed to staying ahead of the curve. With the launch of 'NCIS: New York', the network is demonstrating its ability to adapt and innovate, while also remaining true to its core strengths. As the fall 2026 season approaches, fans of the NCIS franchise will be eagerly anticipating the premiere of 'NCIS: New York', and it will be interesting to see how the show performs in the highly competitive television market. With its unique setting, engaging characters, and built-in audience, 'NCIS: New York' is well-positioned for success, and it will be exciting to see how the series unfolds in the months to come.

Looking to the future, it is clear that 'NCIS: New York' is just the beginning of an exciting new chapter for CBS. As the network continues to expand its franchises and push the boundaries of what is possible in television, it will be interesting to see what other surprises are in store for fans. With its commitment to innovation and excellence, CBS is poised to remain a major player in the television industry, and 'NCIS: New York' is just the latest example of the network's dedication to bringing high-quality programming to audiences around the world. As the television landscape continues to evolve, one thing is certain: 'NCIS: New York' is set to make a big impact, and fans of the franchise will be eagerly anticipating its premiere.
